detailed_analysis: Both scoped files are small spec-factory adapters in Layer 3 (the port/wiring layer per architecture §7, §12 step 2). antigravity.py (46 lines) builds the skill-only AgenticToolSpec for Antigravity; _rules_factory.py (55 lines) is a shared DRY factory producing the per-tool `rules` CustomizationTypeIO cell, consumed by claude.py, codex.py, and opencode.py. Both are well under the 300-line module limit and the 40-line function limit (build_antigravity_spec ~32 LOC, build_global_rules_io ~42 LOC). Naming follows the two-word/role+mechanism convention. No secrets, no injection vectors, no global mutable state, no silent error swallowing, no mutable default arguments. Imports of concrete *_io adapters are performed lazily inside the factory bodies — matching the established codebase convention and keeping module import side-effect free (W5 clean). The wrapper closures (parse_skill/render_skill, parse_rules/render_rules) correctly adapt the ParseFn Protocol (which mandates keyword-only artifact_path/artifact_root) to the narrower underlying parser signatures. Verified parse_antigravity_skill_md and parse_rules_md exist with compatible signatures; GLOBAL_RULE_NAME, extract_pair_id_from_md, RulesFileLayout, and DirectorySkillLayout all resolve. Architecture and spec conformance is clean: the sync engine never references a concrete tool name (the tool name 'antigravity' lives only in this adapter/factory layer per goal 5), the factories produce frozen-dataclass ports, and US-10 AC-2 (new tool = one spec factory + config keys) is honoured. Findings are limited to one minor unused-parameter observation; no CRITICAL, MAJOR, or WARNING-class defects.

findings.problem: The keyword-only parameters `artifact_path` and `artifact_root` are accepted (to satisfy the ParseFn Protocol in agentic_tool_spec.py lines 27-36) but never forwarded to parse_antigravity_skill_md, whose signature does not accept them. This is intentional interface conformance, not a bug — the underlying skill parser derives identity from frontmatter/prior canonical, not from the artifact path. Flagged only as a transparency note: a reader may expect path-based identity resolution (as rules_io.parse_rules_md uses artifact_path) and be surprised it is dropped here. No remediation implied.

checks.note: Each file adds a tool via one spec factory + config keys; no edit to the sync algorithm required.

checks.reference: goal 5 (sync engine never references a concrete tool name)
checks.note: Tool name 'antigravity' appears only in this adapter/factory layer; _rules_factory receives the tool name as a parameter.
Both files sit in Layer 3 (tool_specs/*), depend inward on agentic_tool_spec and concrete *_io adapters; no inward layer imports a use case or framework module.
No cyclic or upward imports; lazy in-body imports avoid import-time coupling.
Produced CustomizationTypeIO bundles wire parse/render/extract_pair_id with a valid file_layout; __post_init__ validation will reject drift.
secret_policy is evaluated by the sync core, not per-adapter (architecture §1 goal 6); these wiring factories carry no egress boundary.

verdict_summary: Both spec-factory files are clean, conform to layering and the tool-name-abstraction invariant, and contain only one INFO-level intentional unused-parameter note.
